title: sterculia guttata
intro: Sterculia guttata, also known as the spotted sterculia or spotted boxwood, is a deciduous tree native to India and Sri Lanka, belonging to the family Malvaceae. The bark and roots of this plant have been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Sterculia guttata include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Sterculia guttata include infusions, decoctions, and powders, which can be used to treat various ailments, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
